title: The Real Trump Resistance Doesn’t Need a Hashtag
description: Expand the bounds of whom you are committed to. Reconsider whom you feel responsible for.
author: Julia Angwin and Ami Fields-Meyer

title: Trump Breaks Up Education Dept., Prompting Worries Over Civil Rights
description: Special education programs and the civil rights office will be moved out of the Education Department, the most aggressive move yet by the Trump administration to dismantle the agency.
author: Michael C. Bender and Dana Goldstein

title: The UFC 250 Was a Garish Spectacle of American Decline
description: Only the hackiest screenwriter imaginable would script America’s debasement this way.
author: Michelle Goldberg

title: In Its Tragic Finale, ‘Romeo and Juliet’ Is Interrupted by a Cat
description: Performers were tested by the unexpected during a ballet production in Turkey.
author: Victor Mather

title: What if Both Parties Redistricted to the Max?
description: How the scramble for partisan advantage could further distort America’s politics.
author: Nathaniel Rakich

title: How Tehran Won the World
description: Iran’s defiance in the face of Western aggression has become a rallying cry.
author: Azadeh Moaveni

title: Frustrated by Courts, Trump Weighed Suspending a Constitutional Right
description: Secret memos show that the White House debated last year, to a greater degree than previously known, whether to limit habeas corpus rights for undocumented immigrants.
author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan

title: Iran Will Enter Nuclear Talks With the Trump Administration Feeling Emboldened
description: Despite military setbacks during the war, Tehran is presenting a narrative of victory before negotiations with Washington.
author: Yeganeh Torbati

title: The Verdict on Biden Is In
description: Preliminary judgments on Joe Biden’s one-term presidency, including Jill Biden’s, are all the rage.
author: Carlos Lozada

title: Just How Much Are E.V. Drivers Saving With Gas Prices Still High?
description: Over a long period, gas prices tend to be more volatile than electricity rates.
author: Francesca Paris

title: Will Commercial Ships Have to Pay to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz? Maybe.
description: Charging a toll is illegal under international law, but some fees are allowed for services. It is not clear what services Iran would provide, but there were no fees charged before the war.
author: Ephrat Livni

title: Algae Is Turning the Reflecting Pool Green. Again.
description: Algal blooms have hit the site, between the Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument, after a $14.2 million repair project.
author: Maxine Joselow

title: Trump Lost The War He Started In Iran
description: The United States is emerging weaker — militarily, diplomatically and economically — than at the start of the war.
author: The Editorial Board

title: How Does One Brain Speak Two Languages?
description: A new study of bilingual speakers suggests that a single “grammatical engine” in the brain can power multiple languages at once.
author: K. R. Callaway

title: Are You a Morning Person or Night Owl? Take Our Sleep Quiz.
description: Think you’re a morning person, a night owl or something in between? Take our “chronotype” quiz to find out.
author: Amanda Schupak and Montse Galbany

title: Father Dionysios Tabakis’s Drone Metal Debut
description: Father Dionysios Tabakis has never played a live concert, but after his first album became a word-of-mouth hit, he’ll be performing at a U.S. festival this summer.
author: Hester Underhill

title: The Pain of Caring for a Parent Who Abused You
description: The United States is reliant on unpaid family caregivers, and millions of adult children are caring for parents who didn’t really care for them.
author: Katie Engelhart

title: Inside Trump’s White House, the Epstein Files Caused a Freakout
description: The president’s top advisers gathered in a series of Situation Room meetings as they struggled to contain a scandal engulfing Donald Trump himself.
author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan

title: U.S. at Fault in Strike on School in Iran, Preliminary Inquiry Says
description: Outdated targeting data may have resulted in a mistaken missile strike, according to the ongoing military investigation, which undercuts President Trump’s assertion that Iran could be to blame.
author: Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt, Tyler Pager, Malachy Browne and Helene Cooper
